title: 10 best herbal teas for psoriasis
intro_desc: Herbal teas for psoriasis are infusions or decoctions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and roots, which can help alleviate psoriasis symptoms. These teas can have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating properties, contributing to the reduction of redness, itching, and scaling associated with psoriasis. Examples of herbal teas that can benefit individuals with psoriasis include chamomile, calendula, burdock root, and milk thistle. By incorporating these teas into their daily routine, psoriasis patients may experience improvements in their quality of life, such as reduced discomfort, fewer flare-ups, and enhanced mental well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with psoriasis because its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness and swelling associated with the condition. The flavonoids present in yarrow tea, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, which contribute to psoriasis flares. Additionally, yarrow's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties may help combat infections that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms, promoting a healthy skin environment and reducing inflammation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow tea contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in yarrow tea may help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of psoriatic lesions.
Wound-healing properties: Yarrow's ability to promote blood clotting and accelerate wound healing may help reduce the appearance of psoriasis symptoms, such as thickened skin and redness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm skin irritated by psoriasis.
Leaves: Yarrow leaves are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing of psoriatic lesions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh yarrow leaves and flowers or use dried material in sufficient quantities for the desired amount of tea.
Combine one teaspoon of dried yarrow or two teaspoons of fresh yarrow with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes, or until the liquid reaches a comfortable drinking temperature.
Strain the tea into another container using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Serve the tea hot or let it cool to room temperature and store it in an airtight container for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with the condition. The tea's mucilages, such as polysaccharides and flavonoids, soothe the skin by forming a protective barrier that prevents moisture loss and irritation. This comforting brew also contains antioxidants that combat free radicals, thereby reducing oxidative stress that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms, allowing for improved skin health and reduced flakiness and itching.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with psoriasis.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in marshmallow tea help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms such as redness, itching, and scaling.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with psoriasis, providing relief from symptoms like pain and stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilages, which help soothe and calm irritated skin associated with psoriasis.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling caused by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Measure accurately one tablespoon of dried marshmallow root per eight ounces water.
Heat the water to boiling in a medium-sized pot.
Reduce heat and let steep for ten to fifteen minutes allowing flavors to meld.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding solids.
Enjoy warm or chilled with honey to taste taking effect within thirty minutes to an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock teas helps with psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with the condition. The tea's ability to stimulate digestion and eliminate toxins also helps to clear waste products from the body, which can contribute to psoriasis symptoms. Additionally, burdock root's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and exacerbate psoriasis flares. By promoting overall health and wellbeing, burdock teas may provide relief for those suffering from psoriasis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock tea's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often linked to psoriasis symptoms such as redness, itching, and scaling.

Immune system modulation: Burdock tea has been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing excessive inflammation and promoting a balanced immune response, which can help alleviate psoriasis symptoms by reducing the activation of immune cells that contribute to the condition.

Gut health support: Burdock tea's prebiotic properties help promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which is essential for a healthy gut-brain axis and may reduce inflammation and improve overall skin health, both of which are important in managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps soothe and calm irritated skin associated with psoriasis.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling caused by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of dried burdock root arctium lappa or fresh roots.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the herbal mixture.
Steep the herbs for 5-7 minutes in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Enjoy your warm burdock tea within 30 minutes of preparation while still hot and fragrant.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with psoriasis because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling. The tea's antioxidants also help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to skin cell damage and inflammation characteristic of psoriasis. Additionally, the tea's flavonoids and polyphenols have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, further alleviating symptoms of this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with psoriasis skin lesions, making it a potential natural remedy for soothing affected areas.

Antioxidant activity: Marigold tea's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to the progression of psoriasis, promoting overall skin health and reducing oxidative stress.

Wound healing properties: The tea's flavonoids and phenolic acids may enhance collagen synthesis, improve tissue repair, and accelerate wound closure, which could be beneficial in treating chronic wounds associated with psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and reducing inflammation associated with psoriasis.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like psoriasis due to their richness in omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula flowers at dawn on a dry day for optimal potency and flavor retention.
Rinse the fresh calendula flowers with filtered water to remove dirt and debris then pat dry gently.
Measure one teaspoon of dried calendula petals or three teaspoons of fresh calendula flowers for every 8 oz cup.
Steep the herbs in boiling water for 5-7 minutes then strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving with honey and lemon to taste or store chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with psoriasis because of its unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and other beneficial compounds. The tea's high content of catechins, particularly EGCG, has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are key contributors to psoriasis symptoms. Additionally, white tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm and soothe irritated skin, reducing redness, scaling, and itching associated with psoriasis. By incorporating white tea into a wellness routine, individuals may experience relief from psoriasis symptoms and improved overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: White tea is rich in antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal white teas like green tea and chamomile contain anti-inflammatory compounds like catechins and apigenin, which may help reduce inflammation and redness associated with psoriasis.

Soften and calm the skin: The soothing and moisturizing properties of herbal white tea, particularly those containing aloe vera or chamomile, can help soften and calm irritated skin affected by psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the white tea plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, making them an effective ingredient in teas for treating psoriasis.

Bark: The bark of the white tea plant contains flavonoids and polyphenols that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve skin health, making it a valuable addition to medicinal teas for psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality white tea leaves from camellia sinensis plants for optimal flavor.
Sort and clean the harvested white tea leaves to remove impurities and stems.
Measure two teaspoons of dried white tea leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the white tea leaves in boiling water for three to five minutes to release flavors.
Strain and discard the tea leaves, then serve the herbal white tea hot or chilled within an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cannabis teas helps with psoriasis because they contain cannabidiol (CBD), a non-psychoactive compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce the inflammation and irritation associated with psoriasis, leading to clearer skin and reduced redness. Additionally, CBD may also help to regulate the body's immune response, which can contribute to the development of psoriasis in some individuals.
remedy_properties: Cannabidiol (CBD): Helps reduce inflammation and relieve itching and pain associated with psoriasis, as CBD has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects.
Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC): Relaxes the immune system's overactive response that leads to psoriasis symptoms, reducing inflammation and discomfort by binding to cannabinoid receptors in the body.
Terpenes (specifically, beta-caryophyllene and limonene): Inhibit pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to psoriasis, and may also improve mood and reduce anxiety related to the condition.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain high levels of cannabidiolic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe skin irritations associated with psoriasis.
Bark: The bark contains compounds like salicylic acid and flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it beneficial for psoriatic skin lesions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cannabis sativa leaves and trim stems for maximum potency retention.
Combine 10-15 grams of dried cannabis flowers with one liter of boiling water.
Steep the mixture in a heat-resistant container for 20-30 minutes to allow active compounds release.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large glass jar, discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool and then refrigerate it at 4°c (39°f) for up to 24 hours before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with the condition. The apigenin present in chamomile tea has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thus reducing inflammation and soothing itchy skin. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant effects can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthier and more balanced skin environment that is less prone to psoriasis flares.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: German Chamomile contains flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in German Chamomile tea help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin inflammation and damage, potentially soothing symptoms of psoriasis.

Calming and relaxing effects: German Chamomile's calming properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for psoriasis flares, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing overall symptom severity.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe and calm skin inflammation associated with psoriasis.
Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes that can help reduce redness and swelling caused by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried german chamomile flowers in the morning for optimal potency.
Measure one tablespoon of dried german chamomile flowers or two tablespoons of loose-leaf fresh flowers per eight ounces.
Heat filtered water to a simmering temperature around 200°f and let it cool for three minutes.
Steep the herb mixture in the hot water for seven to ten minutes, allowing the flavors and properties to infuse.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids and serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory teas helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with the condition. The tea's flavonoids and polyphenols also exhibit antioxidant effects, which may help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, chicory tea has been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthier skin environment, ultimately helping to alleviate symptoms of psoriasis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chicory tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation in the skin, a key symptom of psoriasis.
Antioxidant activity: Chicory tea has antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and exacerbate psoriasis symptoms.

Immunomodulatory effects: Some studies suggest that chicory tea may have immunomodulatory effects, which means it can help regulate the immune system's response to inflammation in the skin, potentially reducing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm psoriatic skin.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried chicory leaves measuring one tablespoon of herb per cup water.
Rinse the harvested chicory leaves with cold running water to remove impurities gently.
Boil one quart of filtered water and steep the chicory leaves for seven to ten minutes patiently.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a heat-resistant pitcher immediately.
Sweeten the tea with honey or lemon to taste and serve hot or chilled within hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Old man's beard teas helps with psoriasis because it contains a unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and nutrients that soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's active ingredients, such as beta-carotene and vitamins A, C, and E, work to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate psoriatic symptoms like redness, itching, and scaling. By promoting healthy skin cell growth and differentiation, this herbal remedy helps to reduce the appearance of psoriasis plaques and lesions, leading to improved overall skin health and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and redness associated with psoriasis.

Antioxidant activity: Old Man's Beard tea has antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by oxidative stress, a contributing factor to psoriasis.

Immunomodulatory effects: The tea contains compounds like sesquiterpenes and diterpenes that have been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and helping to alleviate symptoms of psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, the roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce skin inflammation associated with psoriasis.
Bark: Containing tannins and phenolic acids, the bark has astringent and antiseptic properties that soothe and calm irritated skin affected by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried clematis vitalba flowers and leaves from your preferred source.
Combine the harvested ingredients with 8 oz of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Drink the tea immediately after preparation or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail teas helps with psoriasis because it is rich in silica, a mineral that has been shown to improve skin elasticity and reduce inflammation. The tea also contains other beneficial compounds such as flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. By consuming horsetail tea, individuals with psoriasis may experience reduced redness, itching, and flaking of the skin, leading to improved overall skin health and a better management of their condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Horsetail tea contains flavonoids, saponins, and other compounds that reduce inflammation, which is a key factor in psoriasis development, helping to soothe skin irritation and redness.

Antioxidant properties: Horsetail's high antioxidant content helps protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to psoriasis symptoms like flaking and scaling, promoting healthy skin cell turnover instead.

Immunomodulatory properties: Horsetail tea has been shown to modulate the immune system, helping to regulate an overactive immune response, a common underlying issue in psoriasis, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silicates, which help soothe and calm irritated skin associated with psoriasis.
Stems: High in flavonoids, which possess anti-inflammatory properties to reduce redness and swelling caused by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried horsetail stems equisetum arvense with scissors or pruning shears.
Rinse the harvested horsetail stems with cold running water to remove any debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried horsetail stems or two tablespoons of fresh stems with 8 ounces boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the bioactive compounds to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and serve warm.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat psoriasis with herbal teas is by regularly consuming tea made from chamomile, calendula, or burdock root. These teas have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. Additionally, drinking tea made from milk thistle or dandelion can help support liver function, which is important for overall skin health. It's important to note that while herbal teas can be a helpful complement to a treatment plan, they should not be used as a substitute for medical advice or treatment from a healthcare professional.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to psoriasis that are treated with herbal teas include eczema and dermatitis. Herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and nettle have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness and itching. Burdock root tea is known to purify the blood and help eliminate toxins, which can be beneficial for skin conditions like psoriasis and eczema. Milk thistle tea is also used to treat skin conditions as it supports liver function and helps detoxify the body. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedies.
